sections.label: Section 396
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ διδάσκω · τοῖς κρατοῦσι δʼ εἰκαθεῖν .
sections.translation: I do not , but to bend before the strong .

sections.label: Section 397
sections.original: σὺ ταῦτα θώπευʼ · οὐκ ἐμοὺς τρόπους λέγεις .
sections.translation: Keep your flattery to yourself ; it is not in my character .

sections.label: Section 398
sections.original: καλόν γε μέντοι μὴ ʼξ ἀβουλίας πεσεῖν .
sections.translation: Regardless , it brings no honor to fall through senselessness .

sections.label: Section 399
sections.original: πεσούμεθʼ , εἰ χρή , πατρὶ τιμωρούμενοι .
sections.translation: I will fall , if need be , while honoring my father .

sections.label: Section 400
sections.original: πατὴρ δὲ τούτων , οἶδα , συγγνώμην ἔχει .
sections.translation: But our father , I know , pardons me for this .

sections.label: Section 401
sections.original: ταῦτʼ ἐστὶ τἄπη πρὸς κακῶν ἐπαινέσαι .
sections.translation: It is for cowards to commend such sentiments .

sections.label: Section 402
sections.original: σὺ δʼ οὐχὶ πείσει καὶ συναινέσεις ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: So you will not be persuaded to agree with me ?

sections.label: Section 403
sections.original: οὐ δῆτα · μή πω νοῦ τοσόνδʼ εἴην κενή .
sections.translation: No , indeed ; may I not yet be so devoid of intelligence .

sections.label: Section 404
sections.original: χωρήσομαί τἄρʼ οἷπερ ἐστάλην ὁδοῦ .
sections.translation: Then I will move on to where I was sent .

sections.label: Section 405
sections.original: ποῖ δʼ ἐμπορεύει ; τῷ φέρεις τάδʼ ἔμπυρα ;
sections.translation: And where is it that you go ? For whom do you take these offerings to be burned ?

sections.label: Section 406
sections.original: μήτηρ με πέμπει πατρὶ τυμβεῦσαι χοάς .
sections.translation: Mother sends me with funeral libations for our father .

sections.label: Section 407
sections.original: πῶς εἶπας ; ἦ τῷ δυσμενεστάτῳ βροτῶν ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? For her deadliest enemy ?

sections.label: Section 408
sections.original: ὃν ἔκτανʼ αὐτή · τοῦτο γὰρ λέξαι θέλεις .
sections.translation: Whom she herself killed , no doubt you wanted to say this as well .

sections.label: Section 409
sections.original: ἐκ τοῦ φίλων πεισθεῖσα ; τῷ τοῦτʼ ἤρεσεν ;
sections.translation: What friend persuaded her ? Whose idea was it ?

sections.label: Section 410
sections.original: ἐκ δείματός του νυκτέρου , δοκεῖν ἐμοί ;
sections.translation: The cause , I think , was fear induced by some vision in the night .

sections.label: Section 411
sections.original: ὦ θεοὶ πατρῷοι , συγγένεσθέ γʼ ἀλλὰ νῦν .
sections.translation: My father ’ s Gods ! Stand with me now at last !

sections.label: Section 412
sections.original: ἔχεις τι θάρσος τοῦδε τοῦ τάρβους πέρι ;
sections.translation: Do you find something heartening in this terror ?

sections.label: Section 413
sections.original: εἴ μοι λέγοις τὴν ὄψιν , εἴποιμʼ ἂν τότε .
sections.translation: If you would tell me the vision , then I could answer .

sections.label: Section 414
sections.original: ἀλλʼ οὐ κάτοιδα πλὴν ἐπὶ σμικρὸν φράσαι .
sections.translation: I know no more than a small part of the story .

sections.label: Section 415
sections.original: λέγʼ ἀλλὰ τοῦτο · πολλά τοι σμικροὶ λόγοι
sections.translation: Tell me that , anyway ; a small tale has often before now tripped men up , or set them upright .

sections.label: Section 417
sections.original: λόγος τις αὐτήν ἐστιν εἰσιδεῖν πατρὸς
sections.translation: It is said that she saw the father of you and of me restored to the sunlight and to her company once more . Then he took the scepter —

sections.label: Section 420
sections.original: πῆξαι λαβόντα σκῆπτρον οὑφόρει ποτὲ
sections.translation: once his own , but now carried by Aegisthus — and planted it at the hearth . From it branched upward a flourishing limb , by which the whole land of the Mycenaeans was overshadowed . Such was the tale that I heard told by one who was present

sections.label: Section 425
sections.original: δείκνυσι τοὔναρ , ἔκλυον ἐξηγουμένου .
sections.translation: when she revealed her dream to the Sun-god . More than this I do not know , except that she sent me by reason of this fear of hers . Now , I beg you by our ancestral gods , obey me , and do not fall in your senselessness !

sections.label: Section 430
sections.original: εἰ γάρ μʼ ἀπώσει , σὺν κακῷ μέτει πάλιν .
sections.translation: If you reject me now , it is in misery that you will next seek me out .

sections.label: Section 431
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ φίλη , τούτων μὲν ὧν ἔχεις χεροῖν
sections.translation: Dear sister , let none of these offerings in your hands touch the tomb . For neither divine law nor piety allows you to dedicate funeral gifts or bring libations to our father from his hateful wife .

sections.label: Section 435
sections.original: ἀλλʼ ἢ πνοαῖσιν ἢ βαθυσκαφεῖ κόνει
sections.translation: No ! To the winds with them ! Or cover them in a deep , dusty hole , where not one of them will ever come near our father ’ s resting place . Rather let these treasures be preserved for her below when she dies . Were she not by nature the most audacious

sections.label: Section 440
sections.original: πασῶν ἔβλαστε , τάσδε δυσμενεῖς χοὰς
sections.translation: of all women , she would never at all have tried to pour these ill-willed offerings to the man she killed . Consider whether you believe that the dead in his tomb will welcome this tribute with affection towards her , by whose hand he died dishonored and was mutilated

sections.label: Section 445
sections.original: ἐμασχαλίσθη , κἀπὶ λουτροῖσιν κάρᾳ
sections.translation:
like an enemy ? She , who , as if to wash herself clean , wiped off the bloodstains on his head ? Surely you do not believe that your bringing these things will absolve her of the murder ?
It is not possible . No , be rid of them . Give him instead a lock of your hair ’ s ends , cut from your own head ,

sections.label: Section 450
sections.original: κἀμοῦ ταλαίνης , σμικρὰ μὲν τάδʼ , ἀλλʼ ὅμως
sections.translation: and from wretched me , too , give these gifts , poor as they are , though all I have . Take this hair , not glossy with unguents , and this girdle , decked with no rich ornament . Then fall down and pray that he himself may come in kindness to us from the world below , a helper against our enemies ;

sections.label: Section 455
sections.original: καὶ παῖδʼ Ὀρέστην ἐξ ὑπερτέρας χερὸς
sections.translation:
and that young Orestes may live to set his foot upon our enemies in superior might , so that hereafter we may crown our father ’ s tomb with wealthier hands than those with which we honor him now .
I think , yes , I think that he too had some part

sections.label: Section 460
sections.original: πέμψαι τάδʼ αὐτῇ δυσπρόσοπτʼ ὀνείρατα ·
sections.translation: in sending her these appalling dreams . Still , sister , do yourself this service and help me , and him , too , that most beloved of all men , who rests in Hades ’ domain , our shared father .

sections.label: Section 464
sections.original: πρὸς εὐσέβειαν ἡ κόρη λέγει · σὺ δέ ,
sections.translation: The girl ’ s advice is pious ; you ,

sections.label: Section 465
sections.original: εἰ σωφρονήσεις , ὦ φίλη , δράσεις τάδε .
sections.translation: dear friend , will do what she says , if you are wise .

sections.label: Section 466
sections.original: δράσω · τὸ γὰρ δίκαιον οὐχ ἔχει λόγον
sections.translation: I will . In regard to a just deed , it is unreasonable for two people to argue , but reasonable to jump to action . But , by the gods , my friends , when I attempt this task , let me have your silence ,

sections.label: Section 470
sections.original: ὡς εἰ τάδʼ ἡ τεκοῦσα πεύσεται , πικρὰν
sections.translation: since if my mother hears of it , I believe that this attempt which I will dare shall in the end cause me bitterness . Exit Chrysothemis on the spectators ’ right .

sections.label: Section 1.472
sections.original: εἰ μὴ ʼγὼ παράφρων μάντις ἔφυν καὶ γνώμας
sections.translation: If I am not a deranged prophet and one who lacks wise judgments ,

sections.label: Section 1.475
sections.original: Δίκα δίκαια φερομένα χεροῖν κράτη ·
sections.translation: Justice , the sender of the omen , will come , winning the just victory of her hands ’ might . She will come in pursuit before long , my child . Courage is mine ,

sections.label: Section 1.480
sections.original: ἁδυπνόων κλύουσαν
sections.translation: since I heard just now of this sweet-blowing dream . Never does the lord of the Hellenes , your producer forget ,

sections.label: Section 1.485
sections.original: οὐδʼ ἁ παλαιὰ χαλκόπλακτος ἀμφάκης γένυς ,
sections.translation: nor does the axe of long ago forget , striking with bronze on its jaws , which in most shameless disgrace annihilated him .

sections.label: Section 1.490
sections.original: κρυπτομένα λόχοις
sections.translation: the bronze-shod Erinys . For an unwed , unbetrothed passion for a marriage polluted by murder seized the pair , though divine law forbade it to them .

sections.label: Section 1.495
sections.original: πρὸ τῶνδέ τοί μʼ ἔχει
sections.translation: Therefore I am confident that the portent — a wonder which I will never blame — will draw near to the criminals and conspirators . To be sure , mortal prophecy

sections.label: Section 1.500
sections.original: τοῖς δρῶσι καὶ συνδρῶσιν . ἤ τοι μαντεῖαι βροτῶν
sections.translation: from fearful dreams or divine signs exists no more , if this vision of the night does not find due fulfillment .

sections.label: Section 505
sections.original: πολύπονος ἱππεία ,
sections.translation: what disaster you have brought upon this land ! For ever since Myrtilus sank to rest beneath the waves ,

sections.label: Section 510
sections.original: παγχρύσων δίφρων
sections.translation: hurled to utter destruction from his golden chariot in disgraceful outrage , from that time to this , outrage and its many sorrows

sections.label: Section 515
sections.original: πολύπονος αἰκία .
sections.translation: were never yet gone from this house .

sections.label: Section 516
sections.original: ἀνειμένη μέν , ὡς ἔοικας , αὖ στρέφει ·
sections.translation: You run loose again , it seems , since Aegisthus is not here , who used always to keep you at least from coming out to the gates and shaming your family . But now , since he is absent , you pay

sections.label: Section 520
sections.original: ἐμοῦ γε · καίτοι πολλὰ πρὸς πολλούς με δὴ
sections.translation: me no mind . And yet you have said of me often and to many listeners that I am a rash and unjust tyrant , who violently abuse you and yours . But it is not I who do violence ; I only return the insults that I so often hear from you .

sections.label: Section 525
sections.original: πατὴρ γάρ , οὐδὲν ἄλλο , σοὶ πρόσχημʼ ἀεὶ
sections.translation: Your father — this and nothing else is your constant pretext — was slain by me . Yes , by me . I know it well . I make no denial . Justice took hold of him , not I alone — Justice , whom you ought to have supported , if you had been in your right mind .

sections.label: Section 530
sections.original: ἐπεὶ πατὴρ σὸς οὗτος , ὃν θρηνεῖς ἀεί ,
sections.translation:
For this father of yours whom you constantly bewail alone of all the Greeks had the heart to sacrifice your own blood , your sister , to the gods — he , who , when sowing his seed , felt none of the pains I did when I gave birth .
Come , tell me now , why , or to please whom ,

sections.label: Section 535
sections.original: ἔθυσεν αὐτήν · πότερον Ἀργείων ἐρεῖς ;
sections.translation: did he sacrifice her ? To please the Argives , you will say ? No , they had no right to kill my daughter . Or , if indeed it was for the sake of his brother Menelaus that he killed my child , was he not to pay me the penalty for that ? Did Menelaus not have two children ,

sections.label: Section 540
sections.original: οὓς τῆσδε μᾶλλον εἰκὸς ἦν θνῄσκειν , πατρὸς
sections.translation: who should in fairness have died instead of my daughter , since the father and mother from whom they were sprung had caused that voyage ? Did Hades have some greater desire to feast on my offspring than on hers ? Or had all love of the children of my womb been

sections.label: Section 545
sections.original: παίδων πόθος παρεῖτο , Μενέλεω δʼ ἐνῆν ;
sections.translation: abandoned by their accursed father , while love for the children of Menelaus filled him ? Were these not the marks of a thoughtless and malicious parent ? I think so , even if I differ from your judgment . So , too , would the dead girl speak , if she could find a voice . For myself , then , I view the past without

sections.label: Section 550
sections.original: δύσθυμος · εἰ δὲ σοὶ δοκῶ φρονεῖν κακῶς ,
sections.translation: dismay ; but if you think my attitude criminal , see that your own judgment is just before you blame your neighbor .

sections.label: Section 552
sections.original: ἐρεῖς μὲν οὐχὶ νῦν γέ μʼ ὡς ἄρξασά τι
sections.translation: This time , at least , you cannot say that I first gave you cause for upset and thereby provoked such words from you . But , if you will permit me ,

sections.label: Section 555
sections.original: λέξαιμʼ ἂν ὀρθῶς τῆς κασιγνήτης θʼ ὁμοῦ .
sections.translation: I would gladly declare the truth , on behalf of my dead father and my sister alike .

sections.label: Section 556
sections.original: καὶ μὴν ἐφίημʼ · εἰ δέ μʼ ὧδʼ ἀεὶ λόγους
sections.translation: Certainly I permit you ; and if you always addressed me in such a tone , you would not be difficult to listen to .

sections.label: Section 558
sections.original: καὶ δὴ λέγω σοι . πατέρα φὴς κτεῖναι . τίς ἂν
sections.translation: Then I will speak . You admit that you killed my father . What statement could be more shameful still than that ,

sections.label: Section 560
sections.original: εἴτʼ οὖν δικαίως εἴτε μή ; λέξω δέ σοι
sections.translation:
whether you did it justly or not ? But I will demonstrate to you that you did not justly kill him . No , the persuasion of that wicked man with whom you now sleep dragged you to it .
Ask the huntress Artemis what wrong she punished when she stayed the frequent winds at Aulis ;

sections.label: Section 565
sections.original: ἢ ʼγὼ φράσω · κείνης γὰρ οὐ θέμις μαθεῖν .
sections.translation: or I will tell you , since we may not learn from her . My father , as I have heard , was once hunting in the grove of the goddess , when his footfall flushed a dappled and antlered stag ; he shot it , and chanced to make a certain boast concerning its slaughter .

sections.label: Section 570
sections.original: κἀκ τοῦδε μηνίσασα Λητῴα κόρη
sections.translation: Angered by this , Leto ’ s daughter detained the Greeks so that in requital for the beast ’ s life my father should sacrifice his own daughter . So it was that she was sacrificed , since the fleet had no other release , neither homeward nor to Troy .
